title: New Rules to Stop Tricky Subscriptions
summary: The government is making new laws to help people cancel subscriptions and get refunds more easily. These laws will stop companies from trapping people in subscriptions that renew automatically without them knowing. People will be able to cancel with just one click instead of making many phone calls. They will also get a 14-day cooling off period after a free trial ends or when a yearly subscription renews, so they can get a refund if they forget to cancel. Companies must give clear information and reminders before subscriptions renew. This could save people money each year. A person named Kim shared her story about having trouble canceling an anti-virus subscription and not getting a refund easily. The new laws are expected to start in spring 2027.

getting your money back when you cancel something
a short time after signing up when you can change your mind and cancel
when a subscription automatically continues and charges you without asking
the group of people who make laws for a country

background_read: Subscriptions are services you pay for regularly, like streaming videos or using apps. Sometimes, companies make it hard to cancel, and people get charged without wanting to. The government is creating rules to protect consumers by making cancellation simple and ensuring refunds are available. This helps people save money and avoid frustration.
Article_Structure: WHO: The government and people like Kim
WHAT: New laws to make canceling subscriptions and getting refunds easier
WHERE: In the country where the laws apply
WHY: To stop subscription traps and save people money

perspectives.description: The laws aim to help people avoid unfair charges and get their money back easily.
